hero.title: Takumi Takasuka
hero.description1: All-Round Engineer / Data Scientist / Intelligent Information Science Researcher / Deep Tech Entrepreneur
hero.description2: 2nd Year Master's Student, Science and Technology Innovation Program, Nagaoka University of Technology
hero.description3: Member of Machine Learning Theory Laboratory
hero.extra_info1: In the Machine Learning Theory Laboratory, I research the automatic generation of highly factual weather commentary, using a decomposed architecture that modularizes the tacit knowledge of expert forecasters and feeds it to an LLM.
hero.extra_info2: To bring this research into society, I am building "Personal Cast," an AI weather-forecaster service for individuals, and taking on the challenge of entrepreneurship. My goal is to deliver weather not as a "forecast" but as the "next action" to take.
hero.extra_info3: These efforts have been selected for programs such as the NEDO NEP Pioneer Course and the WNI Foundation's 16th Weather Culture Award. Drawing on the development skills gained through hackathons and lightning talks, I advance research, development, and commercialization as one.

overview: Prioritizing the "accuracy of information delivery" over "predictive precision," I conduct intelligent-information-science research aimed at establishing design principles for intermediate representations in domain-specific data-to-text (D2T) generation, using the automatic generation of nationwide weather commentary written by expert forecasters as the subject.

From meteorological data such as ERA5, three modules — (1) importance weighting of meteorological variables, (2) synoptic pattern classification via Self-Organizing Maps (SOM), and (3) frontal estimation via Swin-Unet — build an intermediate representation, limiting the LLM's role to "verbalization" to ensure factuality. Through diagnostic experiments that systematically manipulate the "accuracy" and "temporal structure" of this representation as independent variables, I clarify their functional relationship with generation quality.

themes.title: Domain-specific D2T / Automatic Weather Commentary Generation
themes.description: A decomposed architecture that generates highly factual weather commentary with an LLM via an intermediate representation built from experts' modularized tacit knowledge.

themes.title: SOM Synoptic Classification / Swin-Unet Frontal Estimation
themes.description: Development of SOM-based synoptic pattern classification with integrated structural-similarity metrics, and Swin-Unet frontal estimation with a physical-continuity loss.

themes.title: Cognitive Load Analysis of Generative-AI Use
themes.description: Empirical analysis of generative-AI utilization strategies in programming through non-invasive biometric cognitive-load measurement.

data.title: AI Research Institute Website
data.description: This is the official website for AI Research Institute operated by LinkX Japan Inc. A simple yet sophisticated design is adopted to enhance corporate credibility.

data.title: NUTMEG-Seeds
data.description: A web application for sharing technical knowledge within "NUTMEG," the organization I belong to. The theme color is orange, NUTMEG's brand color, with meticulous attention to design details.

data.title: Group-manager-2
data.description: This web application is used when registering for the Nagaoka University of Technology festival. It features excellent functionality including PDF export and statistical data display. The design is stylish with a black-based color scheme.

data.title: Communication-AI
data.description: A web application that allows conversation with generative AI. While the conversation system is simple, it emphasizes design and accessibility, with features and architecture designed for all types of users.

data.title: Slack-message-finder
data.description: A web application that retrieves and stores Slack logs in real-time, displaying them on a Slack-like designed page. It features channel hierarchy display and the ability to track a specific user's weekly activity.

data.title: Mentor-management-app
data.description: A web application that supports mentoring systems. It links mentors with mentees and allows tracking of mentee learning records. A simple web application managed by administrators.

data.title: Verification of Weather Map Image Recognition Performance Using ChatGPT
data.authors: Yuki Takano, Shotaro Watanabe, Gendo Kumoi
data.abstract: Weather map recognition is a complex image recognition task that requires two cognitive processes: symbol interpretation and future prediction. Recent advances in multimodal AI have shown the potential to solve such complex tasks. In this study, we verify the recognition performance of weather map images using ChatGPT, a type of multimodal AI, and propose prompt engineering for more accurate recognition. Weather map recognition is a problem that also appears in university entrance examinations. If weather maps can be recognized, automatic grading and question generation become possible, leading to learning support. Furthermore, automatic generation of weather commentary from weather maps can be expected. Therefore, weather map image recognition is an important challenge, but it requires not only recognizing time-series changes in local meteorological data but also recognizing information about large-scale weather patterns such as pressure distributions and future predictions. In this study, we verify the recognition performance of weather map images using ChatGPT4-Vision, a multimodal AI. We investigate whether it can answer questions about weather maps from university entrance exams and whether it can generate weather commentary from weather map images. By conducting multiple experiments with varying tasks and information in the prompts and evaluating the accuracy of the generated commentary, we propose and verify improvements in reading performance through prompt engineering.

data.title: Verification of Weather Comment Generation from Weather Map Images Using ChatGPT
data.authors: Yuki Takano, Shotaro Watanabe, Gendo Kumoi
data.abstract: Weather forecast text (weather comments) is created by meteorologists interpreting visualized information and verbalizing it. Previous research on automatic weather comment generation has two challenges: incorporating large-scale meteorological field information and generating natural sentences. In this study, we perform automatic generation of weather comments from surface weather map images using ChatGPT4-Vision, a multimodal AI. By conducting experiments that vary the tasks indicated in the prompts and the amount of information provided, and verifying the accuracy of the output comments, we explore the applicability of multimodal AI to weather comment generation. The experimental results showed that structuring prompts to clarify instructions and instructing step-by-step execution were effective. Additionally, few-shot learning demonstrated that ChatGPT4-Vision can understand and generalize new concepts and patterns. The results of this research are expected to contribute to the realization of personalized weather forecasts for each individual.
